4.34315 20 6V9.7C20 10.5284 20.6716 11.2 21.5 11.2H22V12.8H21.5C20.6716 12.8 20 13.4716 20 14.3Z"></path></svg>OpenAPI spec</a></div></div></div><p>The 277CA claim acknowledgment indicates whether a claim was accepted or rejected and (if relevant) the reasons for rejection. This endpoint retrieves processed 277CA transactions from Stedi.</p>
<ol>
<li>Call this endpoint with the <code>transactionId</code> of the 277CA you want to retrieve. You can retrieve the transaction ID through webhooks or through Stedi&#x27;s API. <a href="/docs/healthcare/receive-claim-responses#discover-processed-responses">Learn more</a></li>
<li>The endpoint returns the 277CA in JSON format.</li>
</ol><div class="flex items-end justify-between gap-2"><h2 class="flex scroll-m-28 flex-row items-center gap-2 group api-section-heading-title" depth="2" slug="authorizations" id="authorizations"><a leading-[24px]"><p>A unique identifier for the processed 277 transaction within Stedi. This ID is included in the transaction processed event, which you can receive automatically through Stedi <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/configure-webhooks" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">webhooks</a>. You can also retrieve it through the <a href="https://www.stedi.com/docs/healthcare/api-reference/get-poll-transactions" rel="noreferrer noopener" target="_blank">Poll Transactions endpoint</a> or from the transaction&#x27;s details page within the Stedi portal.</p></div></div></div><nav class="api-section-nav"><div class="flex gap-4 items-center"><svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" Objects</span></div></div><div class="prose-no-margin pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>Patient information and the status of claims related to the patient. You can use the <code>claims.claimStatus.referencedTransactionTraceNumber</code> in this object to correlate the 277CA with the original claim.</p>
<p>Some payers batch acknowledgments for multiple claims into a single 277CA. In these cases, the 277CA will contain multiple <code>patientClaimStatusDetails</code> objects, each with its own <code>referencedTransactionTraceNumber</code> for the corresponding claim.</p><details class="[&amp;[open]&gt;summary&gt;.arrow-down-icon]:rotate-180 [&amp;[open]&gt;summary]:rounded-b-none [&amp;[open]]:rounded-b-md relative overflow-visible" pt-4 pb-4 empty:hidden text-[15px] leading-[24px]"><p>The telephone number including the area code (if applicable). Phone numbers are formatted as AAABBBCCCC, where AAA represents the area code, BBB represents the telephone number prefix, and CCCC represents the telephone number. Phone numbers are provided without separators, such as dashes or parentheses. For example, <code>5551123345</code> for <code>555-112-3345</code>.</p></div></div><div class="text-sm border-t openapi-property scroll-mt-32 w-full p-3 border-x bg-fd-card first:rounded-t-none
